findings.evidence: Grep hits: `Regulator.lean:50` has `regulator_eq_one_of_rank_eq_zero`, `:61/:67/:72/:81/:85/:89` have the rank-zero criteria, while the new proofs at `:115-146` are direct compositions of those lemmas plus `.le`/`omega`/`rw`.
findings.fix: Avoid adding these parallel lower-bound entry points; callers can compose `one_le_regulator_of_rank_eq_zero` or `regulator_eq_one_of_rank_eq_zero` with the appropriate rank-zero criterion, or keep only the genuinely central rank-zero lower bound.
findings.issue: The new `one_le_regulator_of_card_infinitePlace_le_one`, `..._eq_one`, `..._finrank_le_one`, `..._finrank_lt_two`, `..._finrank_eq_one`, `..._rat`, and `..._isTotallyComplex_of_finrank_eq_two` declarations are special-case lower-bound wrappers over the existing exact rank-zero regulator API and rank-zero criteria.

summary: The added consumer lower-bound forms are parallel wrappers over the existing rank-zero regulator theorem and rank-zero criteria rather than new reusable API.
